programmes.show_description: Captivating mystery thriller. Jo and Sam struggle to find a link between Xav Palmer and Todd Drake as the discovery of the murder weapon shifts attention to a landowning family.
Dangerous Driving: Enduring '70s British sitcom. The busmen decide to get fit after reading an article which states that drivers have a shorter life expectancy than conductors.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
S.W.A.L.K.: Classic British sitcom. Simon has a female pen pal who he is looking forward to meeting and possibly proposing to, but the reality of the situation is rather different...
The Nursery: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver. Jack and Stan think that Olive is the ideal candidate to become supervisor of the depot's nursery.
In the 5000th episode of the soap, Carl realises it was Lexi who double-crossed him. Zak is encouraged by Belle to tell Lisa he has quit his job, and Pollard chooses his best man.
Lisa struggles to cope at work but is surprised by Pollard's compassion when he gives her some money for lunch. Val's daughter returns, and Jake celebrates his 18th birthday.
Fiz despairs of Kirk and tells Maria he will have to move out. David assures Gail he will look after her when she's discharged and Roy feels the strain of having Becky as a lodger.
Gail returns home from hospital and Tina feels the pressure when a suspicious Audrey quizzes her about her alibi. Fiz and Maria struggle to resolve the dilemma over Kirk.
Appendix Man: An unsolved case returns to haunt Frost when a man is found hanged in his apartment but police records say that the victim died over a year earlier.
The War of the Roses: Mason and Miller tackle prejudice after an attempt is made on a local farmer's life, and David is terrified that Peggy's double dealings have angered a ghost.
Ties That Bind: Aidensfield is targeted by violent robbers, Mason and Rachel's romantic past puts their careers in jeopardy, and a familiar friend bids farewell.
Painted in Blood: Joyce Barnaby uncovers a nest of plots and double-dealings when she finds a woman stabbed to death while painting watercolours of picturesque Midsomer Florey.
Val is kidnapped by the Dingles on her wedding morning after an altercation with Lisa. Also, Marlon is the epitome of stress as he prepares for the judges' arrival.
David panics when he comes across Tina and Audrey in the middle of a heated row. A nervous Leanne reveals her plans to Paul, and Kirk is persuaded to visit his mum in Cyprus.
A nervous Paul sets fire to Valandro's, spurred on by the thought of spending the future with Leanne. Meanwhile, David and Tina are terrified when Gail experiences a flashback.
Deadlier Than the Male: A domestic dispute rages out of control, and Peggy's hold over David is threatened by a new arrival exerting her feminine wiles.
Jobs for the Boys: A latter-day Robin Hood is operating in the area and baffling the police, and Blaketon and Ventress are divided over their latest private investigation.
Second Sight: When Barnaby and Scott investigate the death of a man recently ejected from a pub for fighting, the post-mortem shows that he may have been subject to electric shocks.
Overture: When DC Endeavour Morse investigates the disappearance of a missing Oxford schoolgirl, he soon risks all in a search for justice that will ultimately shape his destiny.
Appendix Man: An unsolved case returns to haunt Frost when a man is found hanged in his apartment but police records say that the victim died over a year earlier.
The Nursery: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver. Jack and Stan think that Olive is the ideal candidate to become supervisor of the depot's nursery.
Shopping from home.
Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?: Classic British sitcom. When Oliver and Simon do the football pools, Simon's dream of winning turns into a nightmare when his numbers come up.
Stan's Room: Enduring '70s British sitcom about a chirpy bus driver. When Stan finds that living at home is cramping his love life, he decides to move out and rent a room.
Val is kidnapped by the Dingles on her wedding morning after an altercation with Lisa. Also, Marlon is the epitome of stress as he prepares for the judges' arrival.
David panics when he comes across Tina and Audrey in the middle of a heated row. A nervous Leanne reveals her plans to Paul, and Kirk is persuaded to visit his mum in Cyprus.
A nervous Paul sets fire to Valandro's, spurred on by the thought of spending the future with Leanne. Meanwhile, David and Tina are terrified when Gail experiences a flashback.
One Man's Meat: Police drama series. Frost investigates the disappearance of a local environmental health officer, and faces domestic problems with the arrival of a new lodger.
Deadlier Than the Male: A domestic dispute rages out of control, and Peggy's hold over David is threatened by a new arrival exerting her feminine wiles.
Jobs for the Boys: A latter-day Robin Hood is operating in the area and baffling the police, and Blaketon and Ventress are divided over their latest private investigation.
A Tale of Two Hamlets: The death of a young film star at a Satanic experience at his family's ancestral pile brings Barnaby and Troy to the feuding hamlets of Upper and Lower Warden.
Laurel still refuses to attend the parental hearing but Ashley gives her a shocking ultimatum. Marlon gets an audition on a TV cookery show, and Andy tells Jo to fire Sam.
Laurel and Ashley are named as Arthur's parents, leaving Mel devastated. Jo is angry with Andy for firing Jake, and Ross becomes jealous when he sees Marlon and Donna together.
Leanne feigns distress at the sight of the burnt-out restaurant, but finds Paul getting a little too close for comfort. David waits anxiously as DC Weller talks to Gail alone.
The truth dawns on Paul when he turns up at Leanne's to find Dan making himself at home there, and a guilty Tina struggles to hide her anguish as Gail has more flashbacks.
My One and Only: The murder of an insurance salesman produces some remarkable suspects, and Miller is surprised by a face from the past.
The Open Door: An unusual robbery puts the locals in peril, and Peggy's new enterprise proves problematic for Ventress.
The Seagull: DCI Vera Stanhope must find the connection between a skeleton discovered underneath the site of a notorious burnt-out nightclub and a present-day murder.
Trove: Crime drama. A baffling suicide, an anguished father searching for his missing daughter, and the robbery of medieval treasures strains the talents of DC Endeavour Morse.
One Man's Meat: Police drama series. Frost investigates the disappearance of a local environmental health officer and faces domestic problems with the arrival of a new lodger.
